Rin, it's been a while. How have you been?
Normal, I guess. I sometimes dream about scuba diving, but that's about it. I'm probably doing better than you though.
I saw the article in the weekly magazine. That Kazu is the worst. I can't believe he exposed what you did in the drawer in such an awful way...
I'm worried about you, Rin. Can I go visit you some time?
...I went back to university, you know.
My life outside is all back to normal. All of it. I just had to redo half a year's worth of courses.
But it's no good. Something's changed inside me.
Even if I read books I like or make small talk with people, I keep thinking that this isn't what I survived for.
Does anything like that ever cross your mind, Rin?
There's been a lot of cheerful news lately, hasn't there?
The beta-noxenis pollution emissions everywhere had been terrible, but they just disappeared one day.
Weird, isn't it? Apparently, the water that was polluted with it just disappeared one day, like it had been...shrunk.
It doesn't matter. I just mentioned it because it was on my mind. Anyway...
If you ever want somebody else's help, just let me know.
...Thanks, I'll keep that in mind. See you around.
